predictions.text: S&P 500 index will close at least 15% below its 2026-02-19 closing high on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Using official S&P Dow Jones Indices daily closes: identify the 2026-02-19 close as the reference high; prediction is true if any close through 2026-06-30 is <= 0.85 * (that reference close).
evidence: A major U.S. stock index entered correction territory, signaling risk-off conditions and potential spillover declines.
Gold suffered its worst weekly drop since 2011, consistent with rapid cross-asset repositioning.
monitoring_signals: Breadth deterioration (advance/decline, new lows) and credit spread widening
Earnings guidance cuts and analyst estimate downgrades
Real yields and USD strength remaining elevated
predictions.rationale: Correction dynamics often overshoot when positioning unwinds and macro/rates uncertainty persists.

predictions.text: CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) will close at or above 35.0 on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: CBOE published VIX daily closing value is >= 35.0 on any trading day through 2026-06-30.
evidence: A major U.S. stock index entered correction territory, indicating heightened volatility risk.
Oil rose amid intensified U.S. military activity around Iran/Strait of Hormuz, increasing tail-risk premia.
monitoring_signals: SPX 1-month implied volatility and skew steepening
Large daily moves (>=2.5%) and gap openings
Geopolitical escalation headlines affecting energy and shipping
predictions.rationale: Equity drawdowns plus geopolitical energy shocks commonly push implied volatility into the mid-30s.

predictions.text: UK 10-year gilt yield will close at or above 5.00% on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Using Bank of England or widely cited benchmark market close (e.g., Tradeweb/Refinitiv as reported by major financial outlets): the UK 10-year government bond yield close is >= 5.00% on any trading day through 2026-06-30.
evidence: UK government borrowing costs hit the highest since 2008 as inflation fears pressured gilts.
monitoring_signals: UK CPI surprises and services inflation persistence
DM term premium repricing and U.S. yields moving higher
UK fiscal announcements and auction demand/cover ratios
predictions.rationale: With yields already at multi-decade highs, modest additional inflation/term-premium repricing could print 5%.

predictions.text: The Federal Reserve will not deliver any target-range cut to the federal funds rate (no reduction in the upper bound) by the end of the 2026-06-30 FOMC meeting.
predictions.verification_criteria: Compare the federal funds target range upper bound on 2026-03-21 versus after each scheduled FOMC decision through 2026-06-30; prediction is true if the upper bound is never reduced.
evidence: Fed Governor Waller urged caution near-term, with cuts only later if data cooperates.
monitoring_signals: Core inflation prints (CPI/PCE) and wage growth measures
FOMC statements/minutes emphasizing patience or upside risks
Futures-implied cuts being pushed out beyond mid-year
predictions.rationale: Recent Fed communication implies a bias to wait for clearer disinflation before cutting.

predictions.text: U.S. Core PCE inflation (price index excluding food and energy) will be reported at or above 3.0% year-over-year in at least one BEA release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Any BEA Personal Income and Outlays release through 2026-06-30 shows Core PCE (excluding food and energy) YoY >= 3.0% (seasonally adjusted annual rates as published).
evidence: Oil prices rose for a fifth straight week amid intensified Middle East activity, elevating inflation risk sentiment.
Fed commentary reflects caution until inflation cools convincingly.
monitoring_signals: Core services inflation momentum (supercore proxies)
Oil and refined-product pass-through into transportation and goods
Monthly core PCE prints >= 0.25% m/m
predictions.rationale: Sticky services and energy-linked second-round effects could keep core PCE near/above 3% in at least one print.

predictions.text: At least one commercial vessel will be reported as struck/damaged by a missile, drone, mine, or explosive incident in (or immediately adjacent to) the Strait of Hormuz or Gulf of Oman, confirmed by UKMTO and/or U.S./UK official statements.
predictions.verification_criteria: UKMTO incident report and/or U.S. Navy/UK MOD public statement confirms a commercial ship was struck/damaged in the defined area during the window through 2026-06-30.
evidence: Oil prices rose amid intensified U.S. military activity around Iran/Strait of Hormuz, elevating shipping risk premia.
Trump signaled continued U.S. operations against Iran, widening escalation risks.
monitoring_signals: UKMTO/IMB incident feeds and advisories
Satellite/OSINT reports of explosions or vessel distress calls
War-risk premium spikes and rerouting/convoy announcements
predictions.rationale: Escalatory dynamics in a constrained chokepoint increase the probability of at least one confirmed strike incident.

predictions.text: Cuba will announce nationwide emergency measures that explicitly cite fuel or electricity shortages and that close schools and/or non-essential workplaces for at least 3 consecutive days.
predictions.verification_criteria: Cuban government (e.g., Council of Ministers, Ministry statements, Granma, or official decrees) publicly announces closures/stand-down affecting schools and/or non-essential workplaces nationwide for >=3 consecutive days, explicitly citing fuel/electricity shortages, by 2026-06-30.
evidence: U.S. said Cuba is barred from receiving Russian oil as tankers head to the island; fuel shortages deepen.
monitoring_signals: Reports of extended nationwide blackouts and diesel shortages
Official statements on fuel distribution/rationing
Port/arrival data for fuel cargoes and refinery utilization
predictions.rationale: Acute fuel constraints commonly force temporary nationwide shutdown measures to conserve diesel and stabilize the grid.

predictions.text: Nvidia will publicly announce a new data-center GPU (or GPU platform) explicitly described as compliant with then-current U.S. export controls for China, and at least one major server OEM will also publicly confirm availability/support for that product.
predictions.verification_criteria: Nvidia press release/blog/earnings materials announce a China-compliant data-center GPU/platform, and a major OEM (e.g., Lenovo, Dell, HPE, Inspur, Supermicro, Gigabyte) issues a press release/product page/filing confirming systems supporting it, by 2026-07-31.
evidence: Export-control compliance risks are being repriced across AI hardware supply chains following smuggling-related allegations.
monitoring_signals: Nvidia product briefings and partner ecosystem announcements
OEM server launch pages referencing new compliant SKUs
U.S. BIS rule updates and licensing signals
predictions.rationale: Demand pull plus tightening controls incentivize a refreshed, explicitly compliant SKU cycle with rapid OEM support.

predictions.text: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) will disclose either (a) appointment of a new independent Chair of the Board or (b) addition of at least two new independent directors.
predictions.verification_criteria: An SMCI SEC filing (8-K/DEF 14A/10-Q/10-K) through 2026-09-30 reports an independent chair appointment OR at least two new independent directors joining the board (as defined by listing rules/company disclosure).
evidence: Super Micro shares plunged after governance-linked news tied to smuggling allegations, increasing pressure for governance changes.
monitoring_signals: Board committee reshuffles and governance policy updates
Auditor, compliance, or export-control program enhancements disclosed
Proxy advisor commentary and shareholder activism signals
predictions.rationale: Market and regulatory pressure after compliance-linked headlines often catalyzes visible governance bolstering.

predictions.text: At least one U.S. House or Senate committee will hold a public hearing explicitly focused on federal preemption/limits on state-level AI regulation, with an official hearing notice and published record.
predictions.verification_criteria: Congressional committee website posts a hearing notice/title/agenda explicitly referencing AI regulation preemption or limiting state AI rules, and a transcript/video/record is published, by 2026-12-31.
evidence: Trump administration unveiled a national AI policy framework aimed at limiting state-level rules.
monitoring_signals: Committee calendars (House Energy & Commerce, Judiciary; Senate Commerce, Judiciary)
Introduction of preemption-related AI bills or discussion drafts
State AG responses and industry coalition lobbying activity
predictions.rationale: A federal push to curb state AI rules is likely to trigger oversight hearings and legislative exploration.
